subject: GRB 241024B: Fermi GBM Final Localization
body:
The Fermi GBM team reports the detection of a likely SHORT GRB

"At 15:31:12.36 UT on 24 October 2024, the Fermi Gamma-ray Burst Monitor (GBM)
triggered and located GRB 241024B (trigger 751476677/241024647).

The on-ground calculated location, using the Fermi GBM trigger data,
is RA = 162.80, Dec = -1.98 (J2000 degrees, equivalent to J2000 10h 51m, -1d 58'),
with a statistical uncertainty of 14.81 degrees.

The angle from the Fermi LAT boresight is 78 degrees.

The skymap can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2024/bn241024647/quicklook/glg_skymap_all_bn241024647.png

The HEALPix FITS file, including the estimated localization systematic, can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2024/bn241024647/quicklook/glg_healpix_all_bn241024647.fit

The GBM light curve can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2024/bn241024647/quicklook/glg_lc_medres34_bn241024647.gif"
